content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: People with reduced mobility or\
  \ disability\n- url: https://www.aena.es/en/people-reduced-mobility-disability.html\n\
  - source: Aena\n- domain: aena.es\n\n## Content\n\n# Assistance for passengers with\
  \ reduced mobility or disability\n\nAena provides a free airport-assistance service\
  \ for passengers with reduced mobility or a disability at airports in its network.\
  \ The service is intended for passengers whose mobility or disability means that\
  \ they need support during the airport journey.\n\nPassengers who need this assistance\
  \ can request it through their airline, through Aena\u2019s online assistance channel,\
  \ or through the Aena information telephone service. Aena states that assistance\
  \ should be requested at least 48 hours before the flight\u2019s scheduled departure\
  \ so the service can be arranged. Passengers should also inform their airline of\
  \ their needs.\n\nThe assistance service supports the passenger\u2019s movement\
  \ through the airport before departure and after arrival. Depending on the journey\
  \ and the passenger\u2019s needs, service staff may help at designated meeting points,\
  \ during movement through the terminal, and during boarding or disembarkation. Passengers\
  \ using the service may receive priority during boarding, subject to the airport\u2019\
  s operational procedures.\n\nThe service is not presented as a general airport escort\
  \ or orientation service for every passenger. Its stated scope is passengers with\
  \ reduced mobility or a disability who need airport assistance because of that condition.\n\
  \nReservations can be made online or by telephone. Aena\u2019s passenger information\
  \ number is (+34) 91 321 10 00. Passengers should check arrangements with their\
  \ airline before travelling to the airport.\n"
metadata: {}
title: People with reduced mobility or disability

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Home page | La Gomera Airport\
  \ | Aena
- url: https://www.aena.es/en/la-gomera.html
- source: Aena
- domain:\
  \ aena.es

## Content

# Welcome to La Gomera Airport

La Gomera Airport is\
  \ identified by the airport code GMZ. The airport’s official Aena page provides\
  \ access to flight information, transport options, passenger guidance, airport services\
  \ and practical travel information.

## Getting to the airport

Passengers can\
  \ reach the airport by bus, taxi or car. The airport page links to the relevant\
  \ transport information, including the local bus connection, taxi information and\
  \ driving guidance.

## Useful passenger information

Aena provides general\
  \ passenger guidance on:

- hand luggage and permitted items;
- required travel\
  \ documentation;
- passenger rights;
- minors travelling alone;
- travel with\
  \ babies, children or during pregnancy;
- travelling with pets and guide dogs;
\
  - information for passengers travelling as patients;
- transport of firearms.
\
  
The homepage also gives access to airport maps, shops and food outlets, car-hire\
  \ information, parking, Wi-Fi and other airport services.

## Airport layout and\
  \ services

La Gomera is a small regional airport. The official homepage is the\
  \ main entry point for checking current airport information and following links\
  \ to the relevant service pages. Passengers who need ordinary orientation can use\
  \ the airport maps, transport pages, service categories and Aena information channels\
  \ to plan their visit.

The homepage lists the airport’s Barrier Free service\
  \ among the available airport services, but detailed eligibility and reservation\
  \ conditions are provided on a separate Aena assistance page.
"

Aena’s **special-assistance service is for passengers with reduced mobility or a disability who need help during their airport journey**. It is not a general escort or orientation service for passengers who are simply unfamiliar with the airport.

Based on your current assistance information, you have requested **general airport orientation** and have not reported a mobility- or disability-related need. Therefore, the service does **not appear to apply to your situation**.

What you should do:

- Use Aena’s **La Gomera Airport (GMZ)** page for airport maps, transport information and passenger guidance: [Aena – La Gomera Airport](https://www.aena.es/en/la-gomera.html).
- Ask airport or airline staff for directions when you arrive.
- Allow extra time before departure, particularly if you need help finding check-in, security or the gate.
- If you actually need assistance because of reduced mobility, a disability, or another condition affecting your ability to navigate the airport, request Aena assistance **at least 48 hours before departure** and also notify your airline. Aena lists its information number as **+34 91 321 10 00**. Details are on [Aena’s assistance page](https://www.aena.es/en/people-reduced-mobility-disability.html).

final_answer:
Aena’s **special-assistance service is for passengers with reduced mobility or a disability who need help during their airport journey**. It is not a general escort or orientation service for passengers who are simply unfamiliar with the airport.

Based on your current assistance information, you have requested **general airport orientation** and have not reported a mobility- or disability-related need. Therefore, the service does **not appear to apply to your situation**.

What you should do:

- Use Aena’s **La Gomera Airport (GMZ)** page for airport maps, transport information and passenger guidance: [Aena – La Gomera Airport](https://www.aena.es/en/la-gomera.html).
- Ask airport or airline staff for directions when you arrive.
- Allow extra time before departure, particularly if you need help finding check-in, security or the gate.
- If you actually need assistance because of reduced mobility, a disability, or another condition affecting your ability to navigate the airport, request Aena assistance **at least 48 hours before departure** and also notify your airline. Aena lists its information number as **+34 91 321 10 00**. Details are on [Aena’s assistance page](https://www.aena.es/en/people-reduced-mobility-disability.html).
